meta.focus: Review of Kanji, Vocabulary, and Grammar from Lessons 17–18.

sections.title: Part 1: Conversation Check
sections.instructions: Read each conversation and select the best answer.

items.title: Conversation 1
items.context: Taro asks Rikizo about his part-time job at a company.

lines.jp: りきぞさん、アルバイトは 午前ですか、午後ですか。
午後です。毎日 会社の 入り口から 入って、社長に 会います。
社長と 会話を しますか。
はい。社長と 日本語で 会話を して、出口から 出ます。

items.question: What does Rikizo do at the company?
choices: He has a conversation with the president in Japanese and then exits
He meets his friends and studies Japanese
He works in the morning and enters through the exit
He speaks with Taro on the phone
items.answer: He has a conversation with the president in Japanese and then exits
items.explanation: Line 4 states 社長と日本語で会話をして、出口から出ます — 'I have a conversation with the president in Japanese and then exit through the exit.' 会話 (N5.17) = conversation: 会 (meet, かい) + 話 (N5.13, speak, わ). して = te-form of する, used as a te-connector (G7) linking the two sequential actions: 'has a conversation AND THEN exits.' 出口 (N5.17) = exit: 出 (exit/out, N5.17) + 口 (opening, N5.17) = でぐち. Line 2 confirms the アルバイト is in the 午後 (afternoon), not the morning, so the third choice is wrong. The student enters through the 入り口 (entrance), not the 出口 (exit).

items.title: Conversation 2
items.context: Yamamoto-sensei teaches Lee to study Japanese by looking and listening.

lines.jp: リーさん、目で 見て、耳で 聞いてください。
わかりました。先生、新聞を 見せてください。
いいですね。毎日 新聞を 読みましょう。
はい。耳で 聞くことも すきですが、目で 読むことが いちばん すきです。

items.question: What does Yamamoto-sensei ask Lee to do?
choices: Look with their eyes and listen with their ears
Show the newspaper to the teacher
items.answer: Look with their eyes and listen with their ears
items.explanation: Line 1 states 目で見て、耳で聞いてください — 'Please look with your eyes and listen with your ears.' 目 (N5.18) = eye (め), 耳 (N5.18) = ear (みみ). で marks the sense organ as the instrument/means: 目で = 'with/using your eyes.' 見て = te-form of 見る (see, ichidan: る→て), used as a te-connector (G7). 聞いてください = te-form of 聞く (hear, godan く→いて) + ください = polite request 'please listen' (G7). Line 2 is Lee asking 見せてください (please show me) — Lee's request to the teacher, not the teacher's instruction. Line 3 is the teacher suggesting 読みましょう (polite volitional, G8: 'let's read').

items.title: Conversation 3 (Casual)
items.context: Rikizo and Ken chat casually after school about a scary newspaper story.

lines.jp: りきぞ、この 新聞 見た？ こわかったよ。
うん、見た。目が いたいね。
足も いたいよ。立ちたくない。
かなしいね。また 見たいけど。

items.question: What does Ken say about the newspaper?
choices: He does not want to read it
He wants to see it again
items.explanation: Line 1: けん says この新聞見た？こわかったよ — 'Did you see this newspaper? It was scary!' こわかった = plain past of こわい (N5.18, scary). i-adjective plain past: remove い, add かった (plain_past_adj form, G9/G10). In casual speech, i-adjectives drop です in the present (こわい, not こわいです) and use plain past without です (こわかった, not こわかったです). この新聞 without を is natural in casual speech — object particle を is often dropped. りきぞ says かなしいね (sad) in Line 4 — but that is りきぞ's own feeling, not what けん said. Line 4's また見たい (want to see again, plain_desire_tai) is also りきぞ speaking.

sections.title: Part 2: Sentence Scramble
sections.instructions: Tap the words in the correct order to build the sentence.

items.q: I enter the company through the entrance at two in the afternoon.
items.explanation: 午後 + 二時 + に (time) + 会社 + の + 入り口 + から (starting point) + 入ります. 入り口 (N5.17) = entrance: 入 (enter) + り + 口 (opening). から after 入り口 (a noun) marks the starting point 'through/from the entrance' (p_kara, G3). 入ります = polite non-past of 入る (to enter, intransitive godan). 午後二時に = 'at 2 p.m.' — the time expression can also come after the destination phrase: 会社の入り口から午後二時に入ります is equally natural. 出口 (でぐち = exit) is the opposite. 出ます = polite non-past of 出る (to exit) — opposite direction. で marks instrument or location of action, not the starting point.

items.q: The company president exits through the exit.
items.explanation: 社長 + は (topic) + 出口 + から (starting point) + 出ます. 社長 (N5.17) = company president: 社 (しゃ, company) + 長 (N5.8, ちょう, chief). 出口 (N5.17) = exit: 出 (exit) + 口 (opening). から after 出口 (a noun) marks the starting point: 出口から = 'from/through the exit.' 出ます = polite non-past of 出る (to exit, intransitive ichidan). Compare: 入り口から入ります (enters through the entrance) — 入り口 and 出口 are a natural antonym pair, as are 入る and 出る. が would mark 社長 as a subject in a new-information context — は marks it as the topic. 入ります = to enter — wrong direction here.

items.q: Please show me the newspaper.
items.explanation: 新聞 + を (object) + 見せてください. 新聞 (N5.18) = newspaper: 新 (N5.8, new, しん) + 聞 (N5.18, hear, ぶん) = しんぶん. 見せてください = te-form of 見せる (to show, N5.18, ichidan: る→て → 見せて) + ください = polite request 'please show' (G7). The three 見 verbs: 見る (to look/see, volitional), 見える (to be visible, spontaneous), 見せる (to show, transitive). を marks 新聞 as the direct object of 見せる. が would mark 新聞 as a subject — wrong role here. 聞いて = te-form of 聞く (listen) — wrong verb. 読んで = te-form of 読む (read) — also wrong for 'show.'

items.q: The entrance can be seen with my eyes.
items.explanation: 目 + で (instrument) + 入り口 + が (subject of 見える) + 見えます. 目 (N5.18) = eye (め). で marks 目 as the means/instrument: 目で = 'using/with one's eyes.' 見えます = polite non-past of 見える (to be visible, N5.18, ichidan). Key grammar: 見える marks the visible thing with が (not を): X が 見える = 'X can be seen / X is visible.' This が is p_ga (subject marker) — not p_ga_but. を would be incorrect for 見える (visible things take が). 耳 (N5.18) = ear — used with 聞く (listen), not 見える. 見せます = polite non-past of 見せる (to show, transitive) — 入り口を見せます means 'show the entrance to someone,' a completely different meaning.

items.q: My foot hurts, so I don't want to stand.
items.explanation: 足 + が (subject) + いたい + から (because) + 立ちたくないです. 足 (N5.18) = foot/leg (あし). いたい (N5.18) = painful (i-adjective). から after the plain adjective form (いたい) marks a reason: いたいから = 'because it hurts' (p_kara_because, G9 — follows verb/adjective). 立ちたくないです = desire-negative of 立つ (N5.18, to stand, godan つ-verb): stem 立ち + たくない + です. 耳 (N5.18) = ear — the wrong body part; foot pain causes reluctance to stand, not ear pain. かなしい (N5.18) = sad — an emotional state, not physical pain. に marks destination or time — wrong particle after いたい.

items.q: Every day I read the newspaper and listen to Japanese.
items.explanation: 毎日 + 新聞 + を (object) + 読んで + 日本語 + を (object) + 聞いています. 読んで = te-form of 読む (read, godan む-verb: む→んで), used as a te-connector (G7) linking the two actions. 聞いています = te-form of 聞く (聞いて) + います = progressive/habitual 'is listening' (G8). 毎日 can float: 新聞を毎日読んで日本語を聞いています is also natural. から marks a starting point — wrong particle after 新聞 or 日本語. 見て = te-form of 見る (see) — the correct verb for 'read' is 読む, not 見る. が would mark 新聞 as a subject — wrong role; it is the direct object of 読む.

items.q: This morning I did a part-time job at the company and met the president.
items.explanation: 午前 + に (time) + 会社 + で (location of action) + アルバイト + を (object) + して + 社長 + に (partner/recipient) + 会いました. 午前 (N5.17) = morning: 午 (noon) + 前 (N5.9, before). して = te-form of する (do), connecting the two actions (G7): 'did the part-time job AND THEN met the president.' 会いました = polite past of 会う (to meet, N5.17, godan う→って). に after 社長 marks the person one meets: 社長に会う = 'meet the president.' 午前に can float to after 会社で: 会社で午前にアルバイトをして... is equally natural. から marks a starting point — not used after 午前 for time-of-day expressions (に is correct). 出て = te-form of 出る (exit) — wrong verb here.

sections.title: Part 3: Reading — A Part-Time Job at the Company
sections.instructions: Read the passage and answer the questions.

passage.jp: わたしは 午後から 会社で アルバイトを しています。
passage.en: I am doing a part-time job at a company from the afternoon.

passage.jp: 毎日、入り口から 入って、社長に 会います。
passage.en: Every day, I enter through the entrance and meet the president.

passage.jp: 社長と 日本語で 会話を します。
passage.en: I have a conversation with the president in Japanese.

passage.jp: 目で 見て、耳で 聞くのが すきです。
passage.en: I like looking with my eyes and listening with my ears.

passage.jp: きのう 足が いたかったですが、社会に 出たいです。
passage.en: My foot hurt yesterday, but I want to enter society.

questions.q: アルバイトは 午前ですか、午後ですか。
choices: アルバイトは ありません。
questions.explanation: Sentence 1 states わたしは午後から会社でアルバイトをしています — 'I am doing a part-time job at a company from the afternoon.' 午後 (N5.17) = afternoon: 午 (noon, on-reading ご) + 後 (N5.9, after, on-reading ご) = ごご. 午前 (N5.17) = morning: 午 + 前 (N5.9, before, on-reading ぜん) = ごぜん. から after 午後 (a noun) marks the starting point: 午後から = 'from the afternoon onward' (p_kara, G3). The narrator's アルバイト begins in the 午後, not 午前.

questions.q: 社長と 何を しますか。
questions.a: 日本語で 会話を します。
choices: 日本語で 会話を します。
入り口から 出ます。
アルバイトを します。
questions.explanation: Sentence 3 states 社長と日本語で会話をします — 'I have a conversation with the president in Japanese.' 会話 (N5.17) = conversation: 会 (meet, かい) + 話 (N5.13, speak, わ). と marks 社長 as the conversation partner (p_to, 'with'). Sentence 2 shows the narrator enters and meets (会います) the president; Sentence 3 shows what they do together. 入り口から出ます would mean 'exits through the entrance' — but 入り口 is for entering (the narrator exits through 出口 in the conversations).

questions.a: 目で 見て、耳で 聞くのが すきです。
choices: 目で 見て、耳で 聞くのが すきです。
午後に アルバイトを するのが すきです。
会社の 入り口に 入るのが すきです。
社長に 会うのが すきです。
questions.explanation: Sentence 4 states 目で見て、耳で聞くのがすきです — 'I like looking with my eyes and listening with my ears.' の nominalizes the verb phrase 見て、耳で聞く (the act of seeing and listening) — it becomes a noun phrase as the subject of すきです, marked by が. 目 (N5.18, め = eye) and 耳 (N5.18, みみ = ear) are the two sense-organ kanji from Lesson 18. The other choices describe activities from other sentences (アルバイト, 入り口, 社長に会う) but none are explicitly stated as the narrator's favourite.

sections.title: Part 4: Kanji Readings
sections.instructions: Select the correct reading for the [bracketed] word.

items.q: 会社の [入り口] に 入ります。
items.explanation: 入り口 (entrance) is read as iriguchi. 入 (enter): on-reading にゅう, kun-reading い/はい. 口 (opening): on-reading こう, kun-reading くち (voiced to ぐち in compounds). 入り口 = the opening through which one enters. Compare: 出口 (でぐち = exit) — same structure but with 出 (exit) instead of 入. いれます is polite non-past of 入れる (to put in, transitive) — a different verb from 入る (to enter, intransitive). にゅうぐち mixes on-reading にゅう with kun-reading くち — not the standard reading of this compound.

items.q: [出口] から 出てください。
items.explanation: 出口 (exit) is read as deguchi. 出 (exit/take out): on-reading しゅつ, kun-reading で/だ. 口 (opening): kun-reading くち (voiced to ぐち in compounds). から after 出口 (a noun) marks the starting point: 出口から = 'from/through the exit' (p_kara, G3). 入り口 (いりぐち = entrance) is the direct antonym — same structure, opposite direction. しゅつぐち uses on-reading しゅつ — while そのmixed reading exists in other words, でぐち is standard here. でくち misses the voiced sound change: 口 becomes ぐち (voiced), not くち, in this compound.

items.q: [午後] に 会社に 入ります。
items.explanation: 午後 (afternoon) is read as gogo. 午 (noon): on-reading ご — 午 has no kun-reading and only appears in compounds: 午後, 午前, 正午. 後 (N5.9, after): on-reading ご. Both kanji use their on-readings: ご + ご = ごご. 午前 (ごぜん = morning) is the antonym: 午 (ご) + 前 (N5.9, before, on-reading ぜん) = 'before noon.' こうご uses a non-existent reading こう for 午. ごうご adds an incorrect prolonged vowel to the first syllable.

items.q: [社長] は 今 会社に います。
items.explanation: 社長 (company president) is read as shachou. 社 (company): on-reading しゃ. 長 (N5.8, chief/long): on-reading ちょう. Both use on-readings: しゃ + ちょう = しゃちょう. Compare: 会長 (かいちょう = chairman) uses 会 (かい, N5.17) + 長 — a similar compound with different kanji. 会社 (かいしゃ = company) uses 会 + 社. 社会 (しゃかい = society) uses 社 + 会 — note how 会社 and 社会 are the same two kanji in reverse order with completely different meanings.

items.q: 会社で [会話] を しました。
items.explanation: 会話 (conversation) is read as kaiwa. 会 (meet/gathering): on-reading かい. 話 (N5.13, speak/story): on-reading わ (in compounds; kun-reading はなし for standalone 'story'). Both use on-readings: かい + わ = かいわ. The N5.17 trio: 会社 (かいしゃ = company), 社会 (しゃかい = society), 会話 (かいわ = conversation) — three words using 会 and/or 社 with completely different meanings. あいわ uses the kun-reading あ(う) for 会 — incorrect for this compound.

items.q: [目] で 見てください。
items.explanation: 目 (eye) is read as me (kun-reading). 目 (もく/ぼく/め) — kun-reading め is used for the body part in everyday speech. On-reading もく appears in compounds: 目的 (もくてき = purpose). On-reading ぼく in 着目 (ちゃくもく = focusing). Compare: 見 (N5.18) = the action kanji 'see/show' — kun-reading み. 目 is the body part (eye), 見 is the action (see). み is the kun-reading of 見, not 目.

items.q: 先生の 話を [聞きました]。
items.explanation: 聞きました (listened) — 聞 is read as ki (kun-reading). 聞 (ぶん/もん/き) — kun-reading き in 聞く. Polite past: 聞き (stem) + ました = 聞きました. 聞く = to hear/listen (godan く-verb). Compare all polite past forms in the choices: 見ました (みました = looked, from 見る N5.18), 書きました (かきました = wrote, from 書く N5.13), 立ちました (たちました = stood up, from 立つ N5.18). On-readings ぶん/もん appear in literary compounds like 新聞 (しんぶん = newspaper, N5.18).

items.q: [足] が いたいです。
items.explanation: 足 (foot/leg) is read as ashi (kun-reading). 足 (そく/あし) — kun-reading あし for the body part in everyday speech. On-reading そく appears in compounds: 満足 (まんぞく = satisfaction), 遠足 (えんそく = field trip, excursion). てい and ふく are not valid readings of 足. 足 refers to both the foot and the leg — context clarifies. Compare: 立つ (N5.18, to stand) — an action performed using the 足. 目 (め = eye) and 耳 (みみ = ear) are the other body-part kanji from N5.18.

items.q: [耳] で 聞いてください。
items.explanation: 耳 (ear) is read as mimi (kun-reading). 耳 (じ/みみ) — kun-reading みみ for the body part. On-reading じ appears in medical compounds: 耳鼻科 (じびか = ear, nose and throat clinic). 耳で聞く = 'listen with the ears' — で marks the ear as the instrument of listening. Compare: 目 (め = eye, N5.18) — another sense-organ kanji from the same lesson. じ is the on-reading, used in formal/compound contexts. め is the reading of 目 (eye) — a different sense organ.

sections.title: Part 5: Context & Vocabulary
sections.instructions: Select the best word to complete the sentence.

items.q: 会社の _____ から 入って、社長に 会いました。（I entered through the _____ and met the president.）
items.explanation: 入り口 (N5.17) = entrance: 入 (enter) + り + 口 (opening). 入り口から = 'from/through the entrance' — から marks the starting point (p_kara, G3). 入って = te-form of 入る (to enter, intransitive godan: る→って), used as a te-connector leading to the next action (G7). 出口 (N5.17) = exit — the opposite of 入り口; you exit through 出口, not enter through it. 社会 (N5.17) = society — a broad concept, not a physical entrance to a building. 午後 (N5.17) = afternoon — a time expression, not a physical location.

items.q: 足が _____ から、立ちたくないです。（My foot hurts, so I don't want to stand.）
items.explanation: いたい (N5.18) = painful — the i-adjective for physical pain: 足がいたい = 'my foot hurts.' から after the plain i-adjective (いたい) gives a reason: いたいから = 'because it hurts' (p_kara_because, G9 — follows verb/adjective). 立ちたくないです = don't want to stand: 立つ (N5.18, godan つ-verb, stem 立ち) + たくないです (desire-negative). こわい (N5.18) = scary — an emotional response, not physical pain. かなしい (N5.18) = sad — also emotional. いい = good — the opposite of painful; いいから = 'because it is good.'

items.q: 会社で 日本語で _____ を します。（We have a conversation at the company in Japanese.）
items.explanation: 会話 (N5.17) = conversation: 会 (meet, かい) + 話 (N5.13, speak, わ). 会話をする = 'to have a conversation' — a compound suru-verb pattern. 日本語で = 'in Japanese' — で marks the language/means used. 社会 (N5.17) = society — the same kanji as 会社 in reverse order; 社会をする is not a standard expression. 入り口 (N5.17) = entrance — a physical location, not an activity. アルバイト (N5.17) = part-time job — an employment activity, not specifically a conversation.

items.q: 新聞を _____ で 読んでいます。（I am reading the newspaper with my eyes.）
items.explanation: 目 (N5.18) = eye (め) — 目で読む = 'read with the eyes.' で marks the instrument/means: Xで = 'by/using X.' 読んでいます = te-form of 読む (godan む-verb: む→んで) + います = progressive 'is reading' (G8). 耳 (N5.18) = ear — 耳で聞く = 'listen with the ears' (you listen with ears, not read). 足 (N5.18) = foot/leg — you don't read with your feet. 手 (N5.13) = hand — 手で書く = 'write by hand' (you write with hands, not read).

sections.title: Part 6: Grammar Check
sections.instructions: Choose the correct particle or form to complete the sentence.

items.q: 会社に ___。（I enter the company.）
items.explanation: 入ります = polite non-past of 入る (to enter, intransitive godan). に marks 会社 as the destination of movement: 会社に入る = 'enter the company.' 出ます = polite non-past of 出る (to exit, intransitive) — same structure, opposite direction: 会社から出る = 'exit from the company.' 入れます = polite non-past of 入れる (to put in, transitive ichidan) — requires an object marked by を: 何かを入れる (put something in). 出します = polite non-past of 出す (to take out, transitive godan) — also transitive and opposite direction. Key distinction: 入る/出る are intransitive; 入れる/出す are transitive and require a direct object.

items.q: 社長に ___、会話を しました。（I met the president and had a conversation.）
items.explanation: 会って = te-form of 会う (to meet, N5.17, godan う-verb: う→って). The て-form connects two sequential past actions: 社長に会って、会話をしました = 'I met the president and (then) had a conversation' (G7 te-connector). しました confirms the whole sequence is in the past. 会います = polite non-past — describes future or habitual meetings, cannot connect to a following past clause. 会いました = polite past — stands alone ('I met the president') but cannot link to the next clause as a connector. 会いましょう = polite volitional 'let's meet' — a suggestion/invitation, not a completed sequence.

items.q: 目で 見て、耳で ___。（Please look with your eyes and listen with your ears.）
items.explanation: 聞いてください = te-form of 聞く + ください = polite request 'please listen' (G7). 聞く (N5.18) = to hear/listen (godan く-verb): く→いて → 聞いて. Pattern: [verb te-form] + ください = polite request. 見て (te-form of 見る) connects as the first action before the main request ください on 聞く. 聞きます = polite statement 'I will listen' — not a request form. 聞きました = past tense 'I listened' — a completed action, not a request. 聞きましょう = polite volitional 'let's listen' (G8) — a joint suggestion, not a direct request to the listener.

items.q: 足が いたいです ___、会社に 行きます。（My foot hurts, but I will go to the company.）
items.explanation: が (p_ga_but, G9) after a polite adjective form (いたいです) is the clause-linking conjunction 'but' — it contrasts two clauses in polite speech. Note: this sentence has two が. The first is p_ga (subject marker: 足が — 'the foot' is the subject of いたい). The second is p_ga_but (clause connector: いたいですが — 'hurts, but'). から after いたいです would create p_kara_because (G9): 'because my foot hurts, I will go' — a reason clause, not a contrast. で marks instrument or location. に marks destination — already used for 会社に行きます.

items.q: 社会に ___、新聞を 読みたいです。（I want to emerge into society and read newspapers.）
items.explanation: 出て = te-form of 出る (to exit/emerge, N5.17, ichidan: る→て). The て-form connects the first action to the desire: 社会に出て、新聞を読みたいです = 'I want to emerge into society and read newspapers' (G7 te-connector + G8 desire). 社会に出る is an idiomatic expression: 'to enter the workforce / emerge into society.' 読みたいです = desire form (G8): 読む stem + たいです. 出ます = polite non-past statement — cannot connect to a たいです desire clause. 出ました = past tense — the action would already be completed. 出ましょう = polite volitional 'let's exit' — a joint suggestion, not a personal desire.
